title: More Nonsense Limerick 84
content:
There was an old man in a tree,
    Whose whiskers were lovely to see;
    But the birds of the air 
    Pluck'd them perfectly bare,
    To make themselves nests in that tree.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Edward Lear delivers a powerful performance in "More Nonsense Limerick 84"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
